action.reasoning: Observation: the one-step lookahead imitation planner regressed badly (0.2153 -> 0.1240) and the run consumed ~31 minutes, so expensive env deepcopy lookahead is not worth it. Analysis: the baseline heuristic was stronger because direct utility/distance greediness matches the environment better; the learned model likely overfit noisy one-step labels and harmed tunnel/room behavior. We need a cheaper, more robust improvement focused on anti-oscillation and frontier commitment while preserving the strong baseline. Decision: revert to a heuristic-first planner, add explicit momentum/commitment, revisit penalties, local frontier-density bonuses, and a fallback target-selection rule; train only a lightweight residual scorer on heuristic labels from a small subset for tie-breaking; locally evaluate both baseline and new checkpoint on a small train subset, then submit eval with the new checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --save_dir checkpoints/hybrid --max_maps 24 --max_steps 48 --epochs 6 && python local_eval.py --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --limit_per_type 6 --output baseline_small_eval.json && python local_eval.py --checkpoint checkpoints/hybrid --limit_per_type 6 --output hybrid_small_eval.json
